subject: Project Management
title: The Waterfall Paper
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Waterfall Paper.

hook.text: In 1970, Winston Royce described sequential software development. He said it was risky.

buildup.text: Requirements → design → coding → testing → deployment, in strict one-way order.

discovery.text: The Defense Department adopted the model as mandatory for all government contracts.

twist.text: They ignored Royce's warning. He actually recommended iterating, not strict one-way flow.

climax.text: For 30 years, the software industry followed a process its inventor said was flawed.

punchline.text: Waterfall's creator said don't do it. Everyone did.

quiz.question: What did Winston Royce actually recommend in his 1970 paper?
options: Iterating between phases, not strict sequential flow
